---
title: "Git and GitHub for health data science"
toc: false
---

A hands-on introduction to Git and GitHub for health data science — covering the core
concepts, practical workflows from first commit to collaborative pull request,
and habits and best practices.

::: {.callout-important}
## Before the workshop
Please complete the [setup instructions](setup.qmd) before the session if you can. The session moves quickly and we won't have much time to troubleshoot installations on the day.
:::

---

## Event details

|  |  |
|--|--|
| **Date** | Monday 20 April 2026 |
| **Time** | 13:30–15:00 |
| **Presenter** | Dr Ewan Carr, King's College London |

---

## What you'll learn

**Part 1 — Concepts and demonstration**

- What Git is and why researchers use it
- The core vocabulary: repositories, commits, staging, push, pull
- How to track changes to your files and write useful commit messages
- How Git and GitHub fit together
- A live demonstration of the full workflow

**Part 2 — Hands-on practice**

- Turning an existing project folder into a Git repository
- Making commits and viewing your history
- Pushing your work to GitHub
- Contributing to a shared repository via a pull request

---

## Timetable

| Time | Session |
|------|---------|
| 13:30 | Welcome |
| 13:35 | **Part 1: Concepts and demonstration** |
| 14:10 | Questions and pre-flight check |
| 14:15 | **Part 2: Hands-on exercises** |
| 14:50 | Collaborative exercise: contributing to a shared repository |
| 15:00 | Close |

---

## Materials

All materials will be available on this site during and after the workshop.

- [Setup instructions](setup.qmd) — install Git, GitHub Desktop, and create a GitHub account
- [Exercises](exercises.qmd) — step-by-step instructions for Hour 2
- Example project — download link on the [exercises page](exercises.qmd)
